key_assumptions.input: Starting free cash flow per share
key_assumptions.source: FY2025 split-adjusted parent PAT per share; FY2026 was 317.33 yen/sh (+131% YoY); use FY25 as mid-cycle croupier anchor
Croupier mid-cycle: Lawrence gate uses FY2025 owner cash, not FY2026 peak success-fee earnings. Price is last thin print, not VWAP.
Base fees track AUM (+2.9% AUM, +17% base fees FY26); mid-cycle owner cash FY25

open_questions: Confirm **live TSE price** and depth; do not use Stooq/Yahoo alone.
Download **yuho** via EDINET E31267 when API key is available; verify segments and non-recurring items.
Reconcile **operating cash flow** vs net income for owner-cash normalization.
Old dive error:** ~¥23/sh on ~300M shares was pre-split / wrong share count; corrected to split-adjusted filing EPS.
thesis: Simplex Financial Holdings Co., Ltd. (7176.T) is a Tokyo-based independent asset manager and ETF sponsor on TSE Prime. **[Fact]** It runs three lines: institutional fund management, listed ETF creation and management (Nikkei, TOPIX, leveraged, inverse, thematic, and Japan-first active ETFs), and an open-innovation layer that connects investors and banks. **[Fact]** The group traces to Simplex Asset Management (founded 1999). **[Fact]** FY2026 (year ended March 2026) consolidated revenue was **¥22.5 billion**, parent net income **¥10.6 billion**, and split-adjusted basic earnings per share **¥317.33** (`7176.T/02_Quarterly/Earnings_Releases/20260528_2026年3_月期_決算短信_日本基準_連結__finfo2026.pdf`). IR materials: http://www.simplexasset.com/sfh/ (holding company site frames this feed).
why_market_wrong: The market may treat 7176.T as an illiquid micro-float while underpricing a **croupier-like** fee stack on Japan ETF and institutional flows. **[Inference]** At the last exchange print (**¥464**, 100 shares, January 2026), mechanical math on **mid-cycle** owner cash looks very cheap versus FY2025 earnings power. **[Inference]** That print is not a continuous liquid market: there is no reliable daily tape, so charts and portfolio backtests understate both risk and the uncertainty of "market price." There is **no dated predictive attribute** in our base case.
